macro_segments.title: Introduction and the Mishnah on Head Covering and Conditional Marriage

micro_segments.title: Opening remarks on Yom HaShoah and transition to new Mishnah
Review of previous Mishnah on head covering and norms of married women
Introducing the Mishnah on conditional marriage (Kiddushin al Tnai)
Explanation of the first clause: marriage on condition of no vows
Explanation of the second clause: marriage without condition, vows discovered
Parallel case of marriage on condition of no blemishes (Mumim)
Definition of blemishes according to priestly disqualifications

macro_segments.title: Gemara's Clarification on Which Vows Disqualify Marriage

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yochanan's teaching: only vows involving self-affliction (Inui Nefesh)
Examples of afflicting vows: no meat, no wine, no fine clothing
Rav Pappa's question: does this apply to explicit condition or implied
Rav Ashi's response: interpretation of 'no vows' depends on reasonable concern

macro_segments.title: Debate of Rav and Shmuel on Conditional Kiddushin Followed by Nisu'in

micro_segments.title: The central question: does condition made at Kiddushin persist through Nisu'in
Rav's position: once Nisu'in occurs, she needs Get even if condition unmet
Shmuel's position: if condition unmet, no Get needed; original Kiddushin void
Abaye's interpretation of Rav: act of intercourse as binding Kiddushin (Ain Adam Oseh)
Explanation: marital intercourse presumes no illicit sexual intent

macro_segments.title: Parallel Case of Minor Girl Continuing Marital Relations

micro_segments.title: Case of minor married off, becomes adult, continues living with husband
Rav's view: continued marital intercourse binds her to first husband
Shmuel's view: no binding without original valid Kiddushin
Why both cases are needed to illustrate the dispute

macro_segments.title: Exploring Conditional Marriage as Solution to Agunah Problem

micro_segments.title: The statement 'Ein Tnai BeNisuin': whether conditions can bind at Nisu'in stage
Tosafot's discussion on conditional marriage solutions to Agunah
Possibility of using Tnai if condition is explicit and repeated
Alternative explanation: secular marriage status affects Beilat Zenut analysis

macro_segments.title: Reconciling Mishnah with Rav and Shmuel's Debate

micro_segments.title: Question: does Mishnah's second clause presuppose condition made in first clause
Rav's answer: second clause is independent case with no condition stated
Shmuel's interpretation: Mishnah should teach conditional case more broadly
Gemara's resolution: Mishnah applies regardless of whether Nisu'in occurred

macro_segments.title: Case of Vows Without Condition: Presumed Disqualification

micro_segments.title: Question: if no condition stated, why assume man rejects vowed wife
Proposal that woman with vows should be Kiddushei Taos (erroneous)
Rabba's response: it is a Safek whether marriage valid or void
Consequence: Get required (stringent on Issura) but Ketubah not paid (lenient on Mamona)

macro_segments.title: Critique of Intermediate Position in Halacha

micro_segments.title: Observation that Gemara does not recognize intermediate level of Hakapadah
Comparison to secular warranty analogy: defect does not void purchase
Why Gemara limits to Safek or Get M'Derabanan, not intermediate liability

macro_segments.title: Rava's Attempt to Limit Rav-Shmuel Debate to Two-Woman Cases

micro_segments.title: Rava's claim: debate applies only when man made condition with one woman, married another
Rava's claim: single woman case with condition should be universally binding
Abaye's challenge: Mishnah deals with one woman, yet we challenged Shmuel with it
Rava's refinement: one woman case is like two women only under special circumstances

macro_segments.title: Baraita on Invalid Kiddushin Followed by Intercourse

micro_segments.title: Baraita case: Kiddushin in error or with invalid item, followed by intercourse
Tannaitic dispute: does intercourse create binding marriage (Rabban Gamliel) or not (R. Yishmael)
Abaye identifies this as same issue as Rav-Shmuel debate
Rabba distinguishes: error (Taos) differs from explicit condition (Tnai)

macro_segments.title: Gemara's Explanation of Taos vs. Tnai in Baraita

micro_segments.title: First view: parties know original Kiddushin invalid, so intercourse intentional as new Kiddushin
Second view: parties unaware of invalidity, so intercourse presumed under original Kiddushin
Conceptual distinction: explicit condition should frame all subsequent intercourse
Case: man says intercourse conditioned on father's consent
First opinion: condition means non-objection; father's silence suffices
Second opinion (R. Yehuda): condition means explicit agreement required
Gemara: this Baraita debate parallels Rav-Shmuel on condition plus intercourse
Rava's conclusion: explicit conditions should always frame outcomes regardless of intercourse

topical_tags.term: Ain Adam Oseh Beilato Beilat Zenut (no one intends illicit relations)
Ktanah (minor girl) growing up and continuation of relations
Ein Tnai BeNisuin (no conditions bind at Nisu'in stage)
